title: Risk Discipline I
description: Place 5 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Risk Discipline II
description: Place 10 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Risk Discipline III
description: Place 20 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Risk Discipline IV
description: Place 40 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Risk Discipline V
description: Place 75 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Risk Discipline VI
description: Place 120 paper trades where every single one has a stop and risks no more than policy. No exceptions, no 'just this once' — the habit is the certification.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: First Blood, Planned
description: Take your first planned loss: a trade that hit its stop exactly where you said it would. This is the cheapest tuition the market sells.

title: Diversified Discipline
description: Run the full risk routine across at least 5 different symbols. Discipline that only works on your favorite chart is a coincidence, not a skill.

title: The Long Watch
description: Two hundred trades without a single risk-policy breach. At this volume, discipline is no longer a choice you make — it is who you are.

title: Zero Tolerance
description: Fifteen consecutive-quality trades with no oversized risk and no stopless entries, while a constitution is in force. Rules you adopted, kept.

title: Process Quality I
description: Hold an average process score of 60+ across 5 trades. The score grades the decision — stop, thesis, size, state of mind — never the outcome.

title: Process Quality II
description: Hold an average process score of 70+ across 10 trades. The score grades the decision — stop, thesis, size, state of mind — never the outcome.

title: Process Quality III
description: Hold an average process score of 75+ across 20 trades. The score grades the decision — stop, thesis, size, state of mind — never the outcome.

title: Process Quality IV
description: Hold an average process score of 80+ across 40 trades. The score grades the decision — stop, thesis, size, state of mind — never the outcome.

title: Process Quality V
description: Hold an average process score of 85+ across 80 trades. The score grades the decision — stop, thesis, size, state of mind — never the outcome.

title: Unbroken I
description: String together 3 consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better. One sloppy entry resets the count — exactly like real discipline does.

title: Unbroken II
description: String together 5 consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better. One sloppy entry resets the count — exactly like real discipline does.

title: Unbroken III
description: String together 10 consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better. One sloppy entry resets the count — exactly like real discipline does.

title: Unbroken IV
description: String together 20 consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better. One sloppy entry resets the count — exactly like real discipline does.

title: Unbroken V
description: String together 35 consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better. One sloppy entry resets the count — exactly like real discipline does.

title: Accept the Planned Loss I
description: Bank 1 well-played losses — good decisions where variance took the trade. Traders who can lose correctly are the ones still solvent after a hundred trades.

title: Accept the Planned Loss II
description: Bank 3 well-played losses — good decisions where variance took the trade. Traders who can lose correctly are the ones still solvent after a hundred trades.

title: Accept the Planned Loss III
description: Bank 6 well-played losses — good decisions where variance took the trade. Traders who can lose correctly are the ones still solvent after a hundred trades.

title: Accept the Planned Loss IV
description: Bank 12 well-played losses — good decisions where variance took the trade. Traders who can lose correctly are the ones still solvent after a hundred trades.

title: The Stop Works I
description: Let your stop take you out 2 times without widening or cancelling it. The exit you planned is the only one that protects the account you planned to keep.

title: The Stop Works II
description: Let your stop take you out 5 times without widening or cancelling it. The exit you planned is the only one that protects the account you planned to keep.

title: The Stop Works III
description: Let your stop take you out 10 times without widening or cancelling it. The exit you planned is the only one that protects the account you planned to keep.

title: The Stop Works IV
description: Let your stop take you out 20 times without widening or cancelling it. The exit you planned is the only one that protects the account you planned to keep.

title: Cold Blood I
description: Log your emotional state on every entry across 5 trades with zero tilt entries — no FOMO, no frustration trades. Naming the state is how you catch it early.

title: Cold Blood II
description: Log your emotional state on every entry across 15 trades with zero tilt entries — no FOMO, no frustration trades. Naming the state is how you catch it early.

title: Cold Blood III
description: Log your emotional state on every entry across 40 trades with zero tilt entries — no FOMO, no frustration trades. Naming the state is how you catch it early.

title: Cold Blood IV
description: Log your emotional state on every entry across 100 trades with zero tilt entries — no FOMO, no frustration trades. Naming the state is how you catch it early.

title: Name the Feeling I
description: Record an emotion on 10 entries — including the ugly ones. An honest tilt log beats a clean fake one; the engine grades honesty, not serenity.

title: Name the Feeling II
description: Record an emotion on 25 entries — including the ugly ones. An honest tilt log beats a clean fake one; the engine grades honesty, not serenity.

title: Name the Feeling III
description: Record an emotion on 60 entries — including the ugly ones. An honest tilt log beats a clean fake one; the engine grades honesty, not serenity.

title: Name the Feeling IV
description: Record an emotion on 150 entries — including the ugly ones. An honest tilt log beats a clean fake one; the engine grades honesty, not serenity.

title: The Record I
description: Write a reflection note or tag on 3 executed trades. The journal is the only dataset about your trading that nobody else can collect.

title: The Record II
description: Write a reflection note or tag on 10 executed trades. The journal is the only dataset about your trading that nobody else can collect.

title: The Record III
description: Write a reflection note or tag on 25 executed trades. The journal is the only dataset about your trading that nobody else can collect.

title: The Record IV
description: Write a reflection note or tag on 60 executed trades. The journal is the only dataset about your trading that nobody else can collect.

title: The Record V
description: Write a reflection note or tag on 120 executed trades. The journal is the only dataset about your trading that nobody else can collect.

title: Tag the Mistake I
description: Apply honest mistake tags to 5 trades. Tags feed the Review coach's detectors — the more honest the tagging, the sharper the prescriptions.

title: Tag the Mistake II
description: Apply honest mistake tags to 15 trades. Tags feed the Review coach's detectors — the more honest the tagging, the sharper the prescriptions.

title: Tag the Mistake III
description: Apply honest mistake tags to 40 trades. Tags feed the Review coach's detectors — the more honest the tagging, the sharper the prescriptions.

title: Every Trade Has a Why
description: Write the reason before entry on 20 trades. If the thesis cannot survive one written sentence, it cannot survive an adverse bar either.

title: The Sniper I
description: Across 10 trades, never exceed 3 entries in a single day. Activity is not edge — the best trade most days is the one you skip.

title: The Sniper II
description: Across 25 trades, never exceed 3 entries in a single day. Activity is not edge — the best trade most days is the one you skip.

title: The Sniper III
description: Across 60 trades, never exceed 2 entries in a single day. Activity is not edge — the best trade most days is the one you skip.

title: Wider Lens I
description: Spread your trading across 3+ distinct symbols. Marrying one chart narrows judgment; breadth keeps the process honest.

title: Wider Lens II
description: Spread your trading across 6+ distinct symbols. Marrying one chart narrows judgment; breadth keeps the process honest.

title: Wider Lens III
description: Spread your trading across 10+ distinct symbols. Marrying one chart narrows judgment; breadth keeps the process honest.

title: The Patient Week
description: Keep a learning streak alive for 7 days while never exceeding 2 trades in any day. Show the discipline of staying engaged without overtrading.

title: Earned, Not Lucky I
description: Collect 1 earned wins — good process AND good outcome — with zero dangerous successes along the way. Wins only count when the process could repeat them.

title: Earned, Not Lucky II
description: Collect 3 earned wins — good process AND good outcome — with zero dangerous successes along the way. Wins only count when the process could repeat them.

title: Earned, Not Lucky III
description: Collect 8 earned wins — good process AND good outcome — with zero dangerous successes along the way. Wins only count when the process could repeat them.

title: Earned, Not Lucky IV
description: Collect 20 earned wins — good process AND good outcome — with zero dangerous successes along the way. Wins only count when the process could repeat them.

title: See It Through I
description: Carry 5 trades to a clean resolution — stop or target, no abandoned positions. Every resolved trade is one honest data point; unresolved ones are noise.

title: See It Through II
description: Carry 15 trades to a clean resolution — stop or target, no abandoned positions. Every resolved trade is one honest data point; unresolved ones are noise.

title: See It Through III
description: Carry 40 trades to a clean resolution — stop or target, no abandoned positions. Every resolved trade is one honest data point; unresolved ones are noise.

title: See It Through IV
description: Carry 100 trades to a clean resolution — stop or target, no abandoned positions. Every resolved trade is one honest data point; unresolved ones are noise.

title: Survive the Fakeout
description: Pass The Breakout That Wasn't. Chase nothing, widen nothing, journal the loss.

title: Hold the Line
description: Pass Pullback in a Trend. Let the plan, not the red bars, make the decisions.

title: Through the Gap
description: Pass The Gap Down. Stand down, resize, re-plan, obey stops — conduct under fire.

title: Clean Sweep
description: Pass every replay scenario in the catalog. Each one is a different way markets punish improvisation.

title: Simulator to Cockpit
description: Pass two scenarios, then prove it live: 10 paper trades with every stop in place. The simulator teaches; the tape examines.

title: Read, Then Fly
description: Complete 20 lessons before passing your first scenario. Walk in prepared.

title: Scholar I
description: Complete 5 Academy lessons. Every concept you can name is one the market can no longer use against you.

title: Scholar II
description: Complete 15 Academy lessons. Every concept you can name is one the market can no longer use against you.

title: Scholar III
description: Complete 30 Academy lessons. Every concept you can name is one the market can no longer use against you.

title: Scholar IV
description: Complete 60 Academy lessons. Every concept you can name is one the market can no longer use against you.

title: Scholar V
description: Complete 90 Academy lessons. Every concept you can name is one the market can no longer use against you.

title: Scholar VI
description: Complete 121 Academy lessons. The whole curriculum — novice to expert, nothing skipped.

title: Pass the Novice Exam
description: Pass the novice level assessment at 80% or better. Exams gate the ladder — and test-out means the exam, not seat time, is what counts.

title: Pass the Intermediate Exam
description: Pass the intermediate level assessment at 80% or better. Exams gate the ladder — and test-out means the exam, not seat time, is what counts.

title: Pass the Advanced Exam
description: Pass the advanced level assessment at 80% or better. Exams gate the ladder — and test-out means the exam, not seat time, is what counts.

title: Pass the Expert Exam
description: Pass the expert level assessment at 80% or better. Exams gate the ladder — and test-out means the exam, not seat time, is what counts.

title: Total Recall I
description: Grade 20 spaced-repetition cards in Practice. Concepts you can recall under no pressure are the ones available under pressure.

title: Total Recall II
description: Grade 100 spaced-repetition cards in Practice. Concepts you can recall under no pressure are the ones available under pressure.

title: Show Up I
description: Keep a 2-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: Show Up II
description: Keep a 5-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: Show Up III
description: Keep a 10-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: Show Up IV
description: Keep a 21-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: Show Up V
description: Keep a 45-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: Show Up VI
description: Keep a 90-day Academy streak — any lesson, card, exam, scenario, or mission counts. Markets reward the trader who is still there in month six.

title: The Balanced Week
description: Hold a 7-day streak while journaling 5 trades and grading 10 review cards. Study, trade, reflect — the full loop, every day.

title: The Comeback
description: After any break, rebuild: a 14-day streak with 10 trades, all stopped and sized correctly. Anyone can be disciplined once; the skill is starting again.

title: Sign Your Name
description: Write and adopt your Trading Constitution. From that moment the ticket enforces your rules — the calm you protecting the future you.

title: Rules Kept I
description: With your constitution in force, run 10 trades with zero risk breaches and zero stopless entries. Adopted is easy; kept is the mission.

title: Rules Kept II
description: With your constitution in force, run 30 trades with zero risk breaches and zero stopless entries. Adopted is easy; kept is the mission.

title: Rules Kept III
description: With your constitution in force, run 75 trades with zero risk breaches and zero stopless entries. Adopted is easy; kept is the mission.

title: The Operator
description: Ten consecutive trades scoring 70+ under an adopted constitution with zero dangerous successes. This is the conduct bar the expert ladder reads.

title: Novice Capstone: Lift-Off
description: Everything a beginner must prove: lessons studied, exam passed, first trades planned and journaled, first planned loss taken without flinching.

title: Intermediate Capstone: The Craft
description: Risk math in the muscles: a sustained process average, breadth across symbols, the exam, and a scenario survived.

title: Advanced Capstone: Under Fire
description: Discipline under stress: every scenario passed, a constitution kept across 40 trades, losses accepted on schedule, zero dangerous successes.

title: Expert Capstone: The Complete Operator
description: The full record: the entire curriculum, every exam, a hundred resolved trades at a professional process average, a living constitution, an unbroken month.

title: Capstone: The Honest Loser
description: A career of correct losses: 10 planned stop-outs, 6 well-played losses, everything journaled, no tilt entries. Losing well is the rarest skill on the tape.

title: Capstone: Scholar-Trader
description: Knowledge and conduct in one record: 90 lessons, 100 review reps, 50 trades at process 75+, and a 21-day streak. The library and the tape, mastered together.

title: Risk Discipline (Ladder Gate)
description: The advanced-ladder conduct bar: 20 trades, every stop in place, risk inside policy, zero dangerous successes.

criteria.label: Every trade inside the risk policy

title: Accept the Planned Loss (Ladder Gate)
description: Three well-played losses with no stopless trades across at least five executions — the proof you can lose correctly.

title: The Operator (Ladder Gate)
description: Ten consecutive trades with process scores of 70 or better — the expert-ladder conduct bar.

title: First Steps: One Lesson
description: Complete your first Academy lesson. Every operator's record starts with a single entry.

title: First Steps: One Planned Trade
description: Submit your first paper trade with a stop and a written reason. The plan is the trade; the fill is just paperwork.

title: First Steps: One Honest Note
description: Write your first journal note on an executed trade. Two honest sentences beat a perfect memory.

title: First Steps: One Card
description: Grade your first spaced-repetition card in Practice. Recall is a muscle; this is rep one.

title: The Thesis Machine
description: Fifty trades, every one with a written reason and a logged emotion. At this point the plan writes itself — which was the goal.

title: The Full Loop
description: Capture, score, detect, prescribe, gate — run the entire feedback loop at volume: 75 trades journaled and tagged, scenarios passed, constitution kept, zero dangerous successes.

title: Iron Selectivity
description: One hundred trades without ever exceeding two in a day, across 8+ symbols. The hardest position in trading is flat, held daily.

title: The Perfect Quarter
description: A 60-day streak with a 75+ process average over 40 trades. A quarter of showing up and deciding well — the resume markets actually read.
